text: Rouen bread prices rose during the winter shortage in Rouen in 1421, and clerks marked the increase in the municipal ledger.
Bakers in Rouen used household bread tokens during January 1422, and each token marked one ration loaf for a registered hearth.
Night barges unloaded grain outside Rouen near Saint Sever gate in 1422, and porters moved sacks before dawn to avoid crowding.
The council ordinance in Rouen recorded that the grain bell was rung after prime in early January 1422.
A revised council ordinance in Rouen recorded that the grain bell was rung after terce in late January 1422.
Citizens in Rouen whispered that merchants withheld grain behind shuttered lofts during the winter of 1422.
Abbey clerks in Rouen recorded wax tally tablets for barley and salt in February 1422, keeping each market debt by household mark.
Sailors in Rouen said the Saint Romain shrine lanterns burned blue before the thaw of 1422.
Rouen bakers were paid in bread scrip during the winter of 1422, and neighbors compared the stamped tokens at the market gate.
Rouen parish beadles were posted at the grain bell in 1422, and witnesses described how they counted households before opening the market.
